channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 keys to defining data sovereignty: Security, privacy, and portability
item.description: Data sovereignty has emerged as a critical concern for businesses and governments, particularly in Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Resurgence of Networks in Digital Transformation
item.description: The network is back at the top of IT priorities, driven by the transformative potential of edge computing and AI's increasing importance.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Taking American businesses global
item.description: The world economy faces a number of challenges right now, from the risks of inflation to geopolitical upheavals. But zoom out to a longer-term view, and the picture looks much rosier.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Digital partnership with a human touch
item.description: Digital infrastructure today is the backbone of enterprise transformation and growth. Optimized and effective digital tools are increasingly the deciding factor between a company powering ahead or withering away.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What comes after search? Cloudflare’s CEO says that’s an ‘existential’ question for the internet
item.description: Google has made it much easier to find the answers we seek without navigating to various websites, but that has made it much harder to do business for media companies and other creators.
item.dc:creator: Anna-Louise Jackson, Fast Company

item.title: Explorers discover wreckage of cargo ship that sank in Lake Superior storm more than 130 years ago
item.description: Explorers have discovered the sunken wreckage of one of the first steel cargo ships to travel the Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the breakup of Google could mean for Apple and other tech companies
item.description: Alphabet may have hoped that Donald Trump winning the White House would quell talk of a Google breakup, but that doesn’t seem likely.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Still using an iPad as a Home Hub? Bad news – Apple is about to end support for it
item.description: If you've been putting off upgrading to the new HomeKit architecture, you'll soon be forced to take the plunge.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: People are paying up for this $39 yogurt that went viral on TikTok
item.description: We’re only in the third month of the year and already there have been a number of bizarre food trends go viral on TikTok—from a $19 strawberry to feeding babies spoonfuls of butter. The latest is a yogurt, called Coconut Cult, that costs $39 for a 16 ounce jar.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Hey Apple! When you’re in a hole, stop digging
item.description: Soon after the non-appearance of the most important Apple Intelligence feature, Apple now finds itself in danger of digging an even deeper hole by promising iOS 19 will be the most significant upgrade in years. The problem: it again runs the risk of promising too much and not delivering enough.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Asus might have just changed the display game for good with three new air-purifying monitors - and one of them is ultrawide
item.description: Asus' new monitors may not include any significant improvements to their display quality, but the Taiwanese company is bringing enhancements to indoor air quality in a unique way.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: South Dakota pushes labels for political deepfake videos ahead of elections
item.description: South Dakota lawmakers have approved legislation that would impose criminal and civil penalties on those who try to use unlabeled deepfakes to influence voters in the state, with the law now awaiting signature from Gov. Larry Rhoden.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Fluance's new active stereo speakers look like a dream soundbar alternative, as well as being perfect for turntables or Bluetooth music
item.description: Audio brand Fluance has announced new bookshelf and tower speakers that could offer a good alternative to your soundbar.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk having ‘great difficulty’ running businesses amid DOGE obligations, Tesla stock slide
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk admitted he is having “great difficulty” running his various businesses as he juggles an advisory role in the Trump administration and leads the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) efforts to overhaul the federal workforce, all while Tesla stocks fall.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Word for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: Word has always been the workhorse app of the Microsoft Office suite. Nearly everyone who uses Office ends up using Word at some point, whether it be for writing memos, typing up agendas, creating reports, crafting business correspondence or any of a thousand other uses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Commvault designa a la veterana de la industria Ha Hoang nueva CIO
item.description: Nueva incorporación a la cúpula directiva de Commvault. El proveedor de soluciones de ciberresiliencia y protección de datos para la nube híbrida ha anunciado el nombramiento de Ha Hoang como su nueva directora de sistemas de la información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Data center vacancies hit historic lows despite record construction
item.description: The supply of data center space in major markets increased by 34% year-over-year to 6,922.6 megawatts in 2024, according to research from CBRE. That rate of growth is considerably higher than the 26% increase seen in 2023, the commercial real estate service provider notes.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Portkey: An open-source AI gateway for easy LLM orchestration
item.description: The explosion of open-source AI frameworks has given developers unprecedented flexibility in deploying AI models. Portkey, an open-source AI gateway, simplifies AI model orchestration by providing a unified API for multiple AI providers, reducing friction in integrating models into applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI driving a 165% rise in data center power demand by 2030
item.description: Global power demand from data centers will increase 50% by 2027 and by as much as 165% by the end of the decade compared with 2023, according to a recent report from Goldman Sachs Research The industry is shrugging off the potential of DeepSeek – which claimed large-scale AI modeling capabilities fr
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4
